mechanism: Both drugs slow heart rate through different mechanisms. Atenolol blocks beta-1 receptors; Verapamil blocks calcium channels. Combined effect increases risk of bradycardia and heart block.
nursingConsiderations: Monitor heart rate >before each dose - hold if < 60 bpm
Check blood pressure - hold if systolic < 100 mmHg
Watch for signs of heart block: dizziness, fatigue, syncope

mechanism: Ibuprofen inhibits platelet function AND increases warfarin levels by displacing it from protein binding. Combined anticoagulant effect increases bleeding risk.
risk: GI bleeding, increased INR, bruising, hemorrhage
nursingConsiderations: Check for signs of bleeding: blood in stool, dark tarry stools, nosebleeds
Monitor INR more frequently when used together
Teach patient to avoid NSAIDs while on warfarin
